system.description:
A small iron canteen, completely sealed by a forge-welded cap, warm to the touch even in the coldest passage. Inside, geothermal water from the Heitfolk vents holds its heat through a Djúpdvergr preservation technique. When the seal is broken, the stored warmth escapes — enough to heat a small shelter for several hours. A personal gift from Hildvarð Bruneson to Kaelen. One use only.

GM Notes

ACQUISITION: Personal gift from Hildvarð Bruneson (Djúpdvergr Forgemaster) to Kaelen specifically, Beat 17. Given as thanks for helping repair the vent network. One canteen total — cannot be duplicated.

MECHANICS (One-Use Consumable):

  • The canteen is forged-welded shut — it cannot be opened normally without destroying it. To use: apply force to the seal (any hero can do this as a free action).
  • On breaking the seal: the geothermal water inside releases its stored warmth in a spreading wave.
  • Effect: Entire small enclosed space (shelter, tent, small cave chamber, 20ft radius) is warmed to comfortable temperature for 4–6 hours. Removes cold-environment penalties within the space. Characters who were hypothermic gain 1 Recovery Test as the warmth stabilizes them.
  • Cannot be used outdoors in open wind — warmth dissipates too quickly.

FICTION: Hildvarð forged this canteen himself. The metal was worked with runes that allow it to hold geothermal heat far longer than physics should allow. He gives it to Kaelen: “You’ll need warmth at some point that matters. Save it.”
